one_time_description: Loss on disposal of tangible capital assets
economic_notes: Revenue from Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership (OFNLP) based on 17% of aggregate provincial gross gaming revenue, restricted to community development, health, education, cultural development and economic development. Sheila Child forever Memorial Economic Development Corporation is a consolidated subsidiary.
notes: Audit is qualified due to inability to observe inventory counts (fuel and gas) at beginning or end of year. Consolidated entity includes Sheila Child forever Memorial Economic Development Corporation. Ottawa Trust Fund of $34,234 held by Government of Canada included as financial asset. No long-term debt outstanding. Loss on disposal of tangible capital assets of $171,149 recorded as other item. Non-compliance with First Nations Financial Transparency Act noted as audit report dated after July 29, 2020 deadline. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $1,795,430 + FNIH $686,543 + Ministry of Northern Development and Mines $199,109 + Ministry of Indigenous Relations and Reconciliation $85,500 = $2,766,582. OSR includes OFNLP $914,197 + Other revenue $462,839 - deferred revenue net adjustment ($85,847) = approximately $1,291,189. Segment expenses for Other ($878,021) includes large infrastructure/capital projects.
